title: card.rs: fetch_card_image no MIME/magic check before decode
description:
LOCATION: src/card.rs:239-254, fetch_card_image()

FINDING: fetch_card_image() downloads up to 5MB and passes the bytes directly to image::load_from_memory() without any MIME sniff or magic byte check. The image crate's load_from_memory will attempt to guess the format and decode it. A remote page can set og:image to a non-image URL (e.g. a large PDF, a ZIP, or a crafted binary), which the image crate will attempt to decode. While image crate errors are caught and logged, there is no size or format pre-filter before the potentially expensive decode attempt.

Additionally, process_image() in media.rs calls with_guessed_format() which sets decode limits, but fetch_card_image() calls image::load_from_memory() directly which does NOT apply the 64MB alloc limit used in media.rs process_image(). A crafted PNG decompression bomb at the og:image URL will not be limited.

IMPACT: A malicious remote page with og:image pointing to a crafted PNG decompression bomb can cause OOM in the background card-fetch task.
close_reason: Fixed: fetch_card_image uses ImageReader with 64MB alloc limit
